housing.summary: Housing is guaranteed for freshmen but not after the first year, which is a significant concern given that off-campus housing in the Miami/Coral Gables area is extremely expensive. Students looking to live off-campus face a challenging and costly rental market. Dorm rules are standard, with restrictions on appliances like rice cookers.
key_quotes: housing isn't guaranteed after the first year, and off campus housing is ridiculously expensive
Anything within a 15-20min drive from campus is either way too expensive or just a crappy apartment
That's a big no-no! [regarding rice cookers in dorms]

social_life.summary: Greek life is active on campus and international students are generally welcomed into fraternities. However, some business fraternities cater heavily to wealthy students, with expensive trips that may exclude lower-income students. The sorority process involves mutual selection and can be intimidating for international students unfamiliar with the culture.
key_quotes: I have pretty average stats and didn't apply for any aid [in context of rushing frats]
Those are only for insanely-rich kids. If you are normal, you won't fit in [regarding business frats]
You are overthinking this. Don't listen to gossip, tv shows...Just go and have fun

dining.summary: There is virtually no discussion of dining halls or campus food in the available posts. The lack of any mention — positive or negative — makes it impossible to assess dining quality. Students seem more focused on the surrounding Miami restaurant scene than on-campus options.
key_quotes: if your family has the money, then it's a good city to live in, but both NY and Miami are very expensive
U of Miami isn't in Miami proper — it's in Coral Gables, south of the main city
the campus is beautiful, huge, and designed for [campus life]

mental_health.summary: Mental health struggles appear in several posts from UM students, particularly around academic pressure and financial stress. One student mentioned lying to their parents about attending college after a mental health crisis worsened by a hurricane. There is no specific mention of UM's mental health services being helpful or unhelpful.
key_quotes: My mental health took a sharp decline as I was in a major that I feel absolutely no passion in whatsoever
I had an incredibly rough time this semester, I had to deal with 2 Hurricanes, The election, and an entirely unrelated mental health crisis
I'm scared that I will feel outcast due to my financial status

financial_aid.summary: UM is widely described as expensive and not generous with financial aid, especially for international students. Financial aid appeals are possible but take months and require strong documentation. The school does offer merit scholarships, but a full ride is rare and the average package for internationals is roughly $25k — far from covering full cost.
key_quotes: UM is notoriously known for expensive tuition
their avg package was around 25k (which is far from a full ride). They have an Honor's scholarship but the cut-off is really high
I appealed. It took months. It did get approved but I had strong documentation supporting financial change in income

academics.summary: Academic advising gets mixed reviews, with one student warning that '90% of the advisors are not helpful.' The cognate system (UM's version of gen eds) confuses many incoming students. Pre-med students debate whether UM's smaller size offers better research opportunities or whether grades are harder to maintain compared to larger state schools.
key_quotes: 90% of the advisors are not helpful
USF is better for pre med. Classes are also less forgiving for maintaining GPA
My son chose UM over USF for pre med because it's smaller and presented more personal opportunities

administration.summary: Administrative processes like transfer applications and waitlist communication are described as slow and opaque, with rolling decisions extending to July. Financial aid processing is lengthy. Students report being confused about basic systems like housing portals and login credentials, suggesting bureaucratic inefficiency.
key_quotes: its rolling until july [for transfer decisions]
I just got in recently as a transfer and wanted to appeal for more aid. I've read that if done by email it usually gets rejected
I called them and they said that my password was expired and that I needed to reset it

location_and_campus.summary: The campus in Coral Gables is consistently praised as beautiful, well-designed, and distinct from a city campus. Being near but not in Miami proper is seen as both a pro (safe, contained campus feel) and a con (need a car for much of Miami). The Metrorail connection to Brickell is a practical commuting option students recommend.
key_quotes: U of Miami isn't in 'Miami' proper — it's in Coral Gables, south of the main city. the campus is beautiful, huge, and designed for [students]
the metro can literally take you from Brickell to campus in 15 minutes
I'm tired of living in Miami, too materialistic and shallow down here

career_outcomes.summary: Career outcomes get limited direct discussion, but UM's name recognition is viewed as decent for certain fields. One alum ended up in finance and another in med school. For music and entertainment, NYC (NYU) is seen as a stronger launchpad than Miami. The career center gets mentioned generally but no specific UM praise stands out.
key_quotes: My two sons...One eventually went to med school and one is now a Financial [professional]
On a career stand point, go to NYU. Being in NYC will be a major advantage trying to break into the music industry
USC's network is one of the best! Not to mention, the name does carry a lot of weight

value_for_money.summary: UM is frequently flagged as overpriced relative to comparable Florida schools. Multiple posts suggest FIU or USF offer similar educational quality at a fraction of the cost, especially for pre-med. The consensus is that UM is only worth it if you receive significant merit aid or can comfortably afford the full sticker price.
key_quotes: Personally find UM overpriced and over hyped. FIU has pretty good scholarships and the quality of professors would be similar to UM
Miami, or any school for that matter, is not worth $200k
Ur going to med school so go to usf and save up for it

overall_vibe.summary: UM has a reputation as a party school for wealthy students, with a 'Sun, fun, and money' vibe that can feel exclusionary to lower-income students. The campus is genuinely beautiful and students seem proud of it, but the 'Harvard of the South' joke captures how the school's self-perception sometimes exceeds its academic reputation. Students who thrive here tend to be comfortable in an expensive, image-conscious environment.
key_quotes: My friend said they're going the Harvard of the south — They're going to the University of Miami
I'm scared that I will feel outcast due to my financial status and that the people here will be snotty and classist
too materialistic and shallow down here [Miami generally]

red_flags.summary: The biggest red flags are cost and financial sustainability: off-campus housing is extremely expensive, financial aid is limited especially for internationals, and the overall Miami lifestyle is costly. There are also concerns about grade deflation hurting pre-med GPAs, and the school's prestige is sometimes seen as inflated relative to its academic rigor and peer institutions.
key_quotes: housing isn't guaranteed after the first year, and off campus housing is ridiculously expensive and general lifestyle here is just super expensive
Classes are also less forgiving for maintaining GPA [compared to state schools]
UM is notoriously known for expensive tuition...their avg package was around 25k (which is far from a full ride)

hidden_gems.summary: UM students get Coral Gables resident rates at the Biltmore Golf Course, a genuine perk in an otherwise expensive city. The library loans out camera equipment, which is a valuable and underutilized resource for creative students. The Metrorail connection makes commuting to Brickell and downtown Miami surprisingly accessible and affordable.
key_quotes: Biltmore golf course. You get coral gables resident rates as a UM student
just recently found out some of the crazy things my school provides such as loaning camera equipment through the library
the metro can literally take you from Brickell to campus in 15 minutes…it is really reliable
